hdarp_metadata.full_caption: Trade Balances in Major Countries, 1960-2009 (Exports/Imports)
theoretical_significance_preview: Contradicts comparative advantage hypothesis that predicts balanced trade through real exchange rate adjustments
Supports classical theory that persistent trade imbalances arise from structural differences in international competitiveness
Demonstrates that free trade does not automatically make nations equally competitive regardless of technology or development levels

notes: Persistent, marked trade imbalances characterize postwar period - neither fixed nor flexible exchang...
Panel 1: Finland, Japan, Korea, Norway, Sweden all move from trade deficits to surpluses over half-c...

hdarp_metadata.full_caption: Real Effective Exchange Rates (PPI-Basis), United States and Japan, 1960-2009
theoretical_significance_preview: Definitively rejects PPP hypothesis at both short-run and long-run levels
Supports classical theory that real exchange rates track real unit labor costs, not stationary equilibrium
Demonstrates non-stationarity is the norm, not deviation from equilibrium
Shows standard theory's prediction of automatic trade balance through exchange rate adjustment does not hold

notes: Real exchange rates are NOT stationary in either short run or long run
Tests based on aggregate price indexes overwhelmingly reject PPP as short-run relationship

hdarp_metadata.full_caption: Law of One Price at the Aggregate Level, United States and Japan, 1960-2009
variables: Deviation from Law of One Price
Adjusted real effective unit labor costs
theoretical_significance_preview: Validates Law of One Price at aggregate level when properly adjusted
Demonstrates real exchange rates gravitate around fundamentals (real unit labor costs adjusted for tradable/nontradable)
Provides superior policy rule to PPP for determining sustainable exchange rate levels
Shows deviations from fundamentals are driven by conjunctural factors (capital flows, policy interventions) not equilibrium adjustments

notes: Appropriate aggregate test of LOP is deviation of real exchange rate from its fundamentals
Given data limitations and anomalous 1980-1987 period, 'remarkable how stable this ratio is over lon...

hdarp_metadata.full_caption: US Balance of Trade, Real Exchange Rate, and Relative GDP, 1960-2009
variables: Relative real GDP (US relative to trading partners)
theoretical_significance_preview: Demonstrates multiple determinants of trade balance: competitiveness (real exchange rate) and relative income (Keynesian channel)
Refutes simplistic attribution of US deficit to exchange rate manipulation by trading partners
Shows improved US competitiveness offset by rising relative income, resulting in worsening deficit
Validates classical theory that trade imbalances reflect real competitive differences, not equilibrium deviations
Integrates classical (cost-based) and Keynesian (income-based) channels in trade determination

notes: Real exchange rate and relative GDP pull in opposite directions on trade balance
Observed deterioration of US trade balance is consistent with observed improvement in its average co...
